name: Anti-uPAR CAR T improved aged-mouse intestinal regeneration
summary: The newest uPAR CAR T aging study extends the animal signal to intestinal barrier, regeneration, immune, and microbiome endpoints.
statement: Therapeutic and prophylactic anti-uPAR CAR T-cell treatment reduced uPAR-positive and senescence-marker-positive intestinal cells and improved aged-mouse barrier function, regenerative capacity, inflammation, mucosal immune function, and microbiome composition.
population_or_model: Aged mice, with human intestinal single-cell data used for target context
time_horizon: Therapeutic and prophylactic aged-mouse treatment windows
quantitative_note: The source reports 0.5 x 10^6 CAR-positive cells in key aged-mouse intestinal experiments, with measurements at 20 days, 6 weeks, and long-term prophylactic follow-up in young-to-aged mice.
caveats: The intervention was not tested in humans.
Human intestinal data in the source support target context, not treatment efficacy.
CAR T persistence and senescent-cell depletion may need tissue- and dose-specific safety controls.
